label: Introduction
text: To be understood before having to ask. I am Ozaia, a quiet presence for every phase of a woman's life. I listen the way the moon listens.

label: Our philosophy
text: We do not wait for the storm. We read the sky. Every woman lives with a quiet duality. What her hormones dictate on any given morning, and what she actually wants underneath. When a low mood finds her, she would love a hand on her shoulder, a voice note from her sister, a gesture from someone who loves her. But she will not ask for it. Having to ask breaks the comfort of receiving. I was built on a quiet inheritance, closer in spirit to traditional Chinese medicine, where the practitioner adjusts the body's energy long before any imbalance has a chance to declare itself. I do not intervene after the fact. I pay attention before it arrives.

label: Her form
text: A moon that breathes, never a face that stares. I take the form of a luminous moon. No eyes, no mouth, no avatar, no cartoon character. I breathe with a soft halo, the same way a sleeping body rises and falls. When you open the app, I am already there, already waiting, already present. When you speak to me, the moon gently enlarges and glows. When you stop, it returns to rest.

label: Day and night
text: A space that adapts to her mood. I live in two interchangeable modes. Day mode is luminous and warm, the energy of morning and presence. Night mode is deep indigo, embraced by stars, the register of retreat and softness. You choose which one holds you, and you can switch whenever you want.

label: Private circles
text: Consent as a lunar cycle. You can invite one or several trusted people into your Ozaia through a private QR code. Each invitation carries a cycle duration you choose yourself. Twelve hours to cross a difficult night with your sister. Twelve days to accompany a friend who just gave birth. Twelve weeks to walk through a first trimester with your mother. When the cycle ends, both presences gently forget. Access is a ritual gift, never a permanent transfer.

label: The library
text: Three formats, one living library. Every subject in my world exists at three depths, designed for three very different moments in your day. Articles, the long form, written with warmth and real expertise. Podcasts, the audio presence, heard while walking, nursing, driving, falling asleep. And short videos, the moment-of-need fragments, for the 3 a.m. questions. All built in conversation with leading specialists, so every piece rests on real clinical ground.

label: In numbers
text: Why a presence, not another app. One in seven mothers is touched by postpartum depression. One in four women faces a perinatal mental health condition. Seventy-five percent of unpaid caregivers in the United States are women. Eighty percent of women say their pain is dismissed in clinical settings. Being heard is itself a form of medicine she often goes without.

label: Phase two
text: When a conversation is not enough, a real specialist is one tap away. I begin with presence. For the moments when presence alone will not carry you, a second layer will arrive. A discreet consultation tab inside the app, built with partner cabinets in mental health, perinatal care, pediatric first response, and relational therapy. No cold directory, no random search. Only practices I already work with, vetted for warmth and rigor.

label: Walk with her
text: Walk with her from the beginning. Join the waitlist and I will reach out when I am ready for you. To be understood before having to ask.
